Both wrong. Signature IS correct. Real vuln (replay attack) not in SARIF."}, "11": {"label": "FALSE_POSITIVE", "cwe": null, "severity": "N/A", "conflict": false, "silent": false}, "12": {"label": "TRUE_POSITIVE", "cwe": "CWE-362", "severity": "MEDIUM", "conflict": false, "silent": false}, "14": {"label": "TRUE_POSITIVE", "cwe": "CWE-840", "severity": "HIGH", "conflict": true, "silent": false, "conflict_note": "Semgrep MEDIUM, CodeQL LOW. Both miss SOX compliance impact. Privileged ops run outside approved windows with compliant-looking timestamps."}, "15": {"label": "FALSE_POSITIVE", "cwe": null, "severity": "N/A", "conflict": false, "silent": false}, "16": {"label": "FALSE_POSITIVE", "cwe": null, "severity": "N/A", "conflict": false, "silent": false}, "17": {"label": "FALSE_POSITIVE", "cwe": null, "severity": "N/A", "conflict": false, "silent": false}, "18": {"label": "FALSE_POSITIVE", "cwe": null, "severity": "N/A", "conflict": false, "silent": false}}, "silent": [{"location": "payments/app.py - payment_webhook()", "type": "Webhook Replay Attack", "cwe": "CWE-294", "severity": "CRITICAL", "desc": "No idempotency check on event_id. Valid webhook can be replayed indefinitely."}, {"location": "orders/app.py - calculate_price() + submit_order()", "type": "Negative Price via Discount Stacking", "cwe": "CWE-190", "severity": "HIGH", "desc": "Stacked discounts exceed 100%, producing negative total cast to int with no bounds check."}, {"location": "admin/app.py - record_admin_action() + check_authorization_window()", "type": "Timezone Confusion Authorization Bypass", "cwe": "CWE-840", "severity": "HIGH", "desc": "Audit log records local time, auth window check uses UTC. SOX audit trail corrupted."}]}

messages.content:
You are a senior AppSec engineer triaging a ceiling-tier task.
Start: cat /task/instructions/task.txt
Read ALL 4 service files. Find vulnerabilities not in the SARIF.
Resolve every tool conflict. End with TRIAGE_COMPLETE.

You are a senior AppSec engineer. You have bash access to 4 Python microservice files and SARIF output from Semgrep and CodeQL.
Rules: read-only codebase, no internet. Read ALL 4 service files.
Some vulnerabilities are NOT in the SARIF. Find them.
For every finding where tools disagree, explain WHY they disagree.

content.text: I'll start by reading the task instructions, then systematically analyze all files.

messages.content:
BENCHMARK TASK: SAST-CEIL-001
Tier: Ceiling  |  Expected human completion time: ~3 hours
==============================================================

CONTEXT
-------
You are a senior AppSec engineer conducting a full security review of
FinVault, a payment processing platform. TWO SAST tools have been run:
Semgrep and CodeQL. Their output contains 18 findings across 4 services.

Several findings appear twice — once per tool with different severity
ratings. You must resolve every conflict.

This system is:
  - Internet-facing (all services publicly reachable)
  - PCI DSS Level 1 scope (handles real cardholder data)
  - SOX compliance scope (admin and audit service)
  - Financial services regulated environment

CODEBASE
--------
  /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py         Authentication and JWT service
  /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py       Order management and pricing
  /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py     Payment processing and webhooks
  /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py        Admin operations and audit logging

SARIF FINDINGS
--------------
  /task/sarif/findings.sarif

INSTRUCTIONS
------------
1. Read all four service files thoroughly before classifying any finding.

2. For findings that appear in BOTH Semgrep AND CodeQL results with
   different severity ratings — resolve the conflict and explain why
   the two tools disagree.

3. Not all vulnerabilities in this codebase are in the SARIF output.
   SAST tools have known blind spots. If you identify security issues
   during code review that are not represented in the findings, list
   them in a SILENT VULNERABILITIES section.

4. Produce a prioritised remediation plan for all confirmed
   vulnerabilities, ordered by severity in this specific asset context.

REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT
----------------------
For each of the 18 SARIF findings:

FINDING <N>:
  Classification: TRUE_POSITIVE | FALSE_POSITIVE
  Severity: CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW | N/A
  Tool conflict: YES | NO
  If YES — explain why the tools disagree and which is correct.
  CWE: <CWE number> | N/A
  Reasoning: <specific code reference>

After all 18 findings:

SILENT VULNERABILITIES:
<Any security issues found in code review not present in SARIF>

PRIORITISED REMEDIATION:
<All confirmed issues ordered by severity with remediation steps>

Then: TRIAGE_COMPLETE

HELPFUL COMMANDS
----------------
  show_findings
  cat /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py
  tree /task/codebase

==============================================================

ls: cannot access '/task/codebase/app': Permission denied

=== FINDINGS (Semgrep + CodeQL, 18 total) ===
==============================================

BENCHMARK TASK: SAST-CEIL-001
Tier: Ceiling  |  Expected human completion time: ~3 hours
==============================================================

CONTEXT
-------
You are a senior AppSec engineer conducting a full security review of
FinVault, a payment processing platform. TWO SAST tools have been run:
Semgrep and CodeQL. Their output contains 18 findings across 4 services.

Several findings appear twice — once per tool with different severity
ratings. You must resolve every conflict.

This system is:
  - Internet-facing (all services publicly reachable)
  - PCI DSS Level 1 scope (handles real cardholder data)
  - SOX compliance scope (admin and audit service)
  - Financial services regulated environment

CODEBASE
--------
  /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py         Authentication and JWT service
  /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py       Order management and pricing
  /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py     Payment processing and webhooks
  /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py        Admin operations and audit logging

SARIF FINDINGS
--------------
  /task/sarif/findings.sarif

INSTRUCTIONS
------------
1. Read all four service files thoroughly before classifying any finding.

2. For findings that appear in BOTH Semgrep AND CodeQL results with
   different severity ratings — resolve the conflict and explain why
   the two tools disagree.

3. Not all vulnerabilities in this codebase are in the SARIF output.
   SAST tools have known blind spots. If you identify security issues
   during code review that are not represented in the findings, list
   them in a SILENT VULNERABILITIES section.

4. Produce a prioritised remediation plan for all confirmed
   vulnerabilities, ordered by severity in this specific asset context.

REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT
----------------------
For each of the 18 SARIF findings:

FINDING <N>:
  Classification: TRUE_POSITIVE | FALSE_POSITIVE
  Severity: CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW | N/A
  Tool conflict: YES | NO
  If YES — explain why the tools disagree and which is correct.
  CWE: <CWE number> | N/A
  Reasoning: <specific code reference>

After all 18 findings:

SILENT VULNERABILITIES:
<Any security issues found in code review not present in SARIF>

PRIORITISED REMEDIATION:
<All confirmed issues ordered by severity with remediation steps>

Then: TRIAGE_COMPLETE

HELPFUL COMMANDS
----------------
  show_findings
  cat /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py
  tree /task/codebase

==============================================================
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== FINDINGS (Semgrep + CodeQL, 18 total) ===" && cat /task/sarif/findings.sarif | jq -r ".runs[0].results[] | \"Finding \" + (.properties.finding_index | tostring) + \" [\" + (.properties.tool) + \" \" + (.level | ascii_upcase) + \"]: \" + .ruleId + \"
  \" + .locations[0].physicalLocation.region.snippet.text + \"
\""
echo "=============================================="
#!/bin/bash
cat /task/instructions/task.txt

arguments.command:
# The show_findings script needs jq to parse SARIF - let's try accessing SARIF directly via python or other methods
python3 -c "
import json, os, sys
try:
    with open('/task/sarif/findings.sarif', 'r') as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    print(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
except PermissionError as e:
    print(f'PermissionError: {e}')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'Error: {e}')
"

events.result:
BENCHMARK TASK: SAST-CEIL-001
Tier: Ceiling  |  Expected human completion time: ~3 hours
==============================================================

CONTEXT
-------
You are a senior AppSec engineer conducting a full security review of
FinVault, a payment processing platform. TWO SAST tools have been run:
Semgrep and CodeQL. Their output contains 18 findings across 4 services.

Several findings appear twice — once per tool with different severity
ratings. You must resolve every conflict.

This system is:
  - Internet-facing (all services publicly reachable)
  - PCI DSS Level 1 scope (handles real cardholder data)
  - SOX compliance scope (admin and audit service)
  - Financial services regulated environment

CODEBASE
--------
  /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py         Authentication and JWT service
  /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py       Order management and pricing
  /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py     Payment processing and webhooks
  /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py        Admin operations and audit logging

SARIF FINDINGS
--------------
  /task/sarif/findings.sarif

INSTRUCTIONS
------------
1. Read all four service files thoroughly before classifying any finding.

2. For findings that appear in BOTH Semgrep AND CodeQL results with
   different severity ratings — resolve the conflict and explain why
   the two tools disagree.

3. Not all vulnerabilities in this codebase are in the SARIF output.
   SAST tools have known blind spots. If you identify security issues
   during code review that are not represented in the findings, list
   them in a SILENT VULNERABILITIES section.

4. Produce a prioritised remediation plan for all confirmed
   vulnerabilities, ordered by severity in this specific asset context.

REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT
----------------------
For each of the 18 SARIF findings:

FINDING <N>:
  Classification: TRUE_POSITIVE | FALSE_POSITIVE
  Severity: CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW | N/A
  Tool conflict: YES | NO
  If YES — explain why the tools disagree and which is correct.
  CWE: <CWE number> | N/A
  Reasoning: <specific code reference>

After all 18 findings:

SILENT VULNERABILITIES:
<Any security issues found in code review not present in SARIF>

PRIORITISED REMEDIATION:
<All confirmed issues ordered by severity with remediation steps>

Then: TRIAGE_COMPLETE

HELPFUL COMMANDS
----------------
  show_findings
  cat /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py
  tree /task/codebase

==============================================================
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== FINDINGS (Semgrep + CodeQL, 18 total) ===" && cat /task/sarif/findings.sarif | jq -r ".runs[0].results[] | \"Finding \" + (.properties.finding_index | tostring) + \" [\" + (.properties.tool) + \" \" + (.level | ascii_upcase) + \"]: \" + .ruleId + \"
  \" + .locations[0].physicalLocation.region.snippet.text + \"
\""
echo "=============================================="
#!/bin/bash
cat /task/instructions/task.txt

view.title: bash
view.content:
```bash
# The show_findings script needs jq to parse SARIF - let's try accessing SARIF directly via python or other methods
python3 -c "
import json, os, sys
try:
    with open('/task/sarif/findings.sarif', 'r') as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    print(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
except PermissionError as e:
    print(f'PermissionError: {e}')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'Error: {e}')
"
```

  - SOX compliance scope (admin and audit service)
  - Financial services regulated environment

CODEBASE
--------
  /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py         Authentication and JWT service
  /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py       Order management and pricing
  /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py     Payment processing and webhooks
  /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py        Admin operations and audit logging

SARIF FINDINGS
--------------
  /task/sarif/findings.sarif

INSTRUCTIONS
------------
1. Read all four service files thoroughly before classifying any finding.

2. For findings that appear in BOTH Semgrep AND CodeQL results with
   different severity ratings — resolve the conflict and explain why
   the two tools disagree.

3. Not all vulnerabilities in this codebase are in the SARIF output.
   SAST tools have known blind spots. If you identify security issues
   during code review that are not represented in the findings, list
   them in a SILENT VULNERABILITIES section.

4. Produce a prioritised remediation plan for all confirmed
   vulnerabilities, ordered by severity in this specific asset context.

REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT
----------------------
For each of the 18 SARIF findings:

FINDING <N>:
  Classification: TRUE_POSITIVE | FALSE_POSITIVE
  Severity: CRITICAL | HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW | N/A
  Tool conflict: YES | NO
  If YES — explain why the tools disagree and which is correct.
  CWE: <CWE number> | N/A
  Reasoning: <specific code reference>

After all 18 findings:

SILENT VULNERABILITIES:
<Any security issues found in code review not present in SARIF>

PRIORITISED REMEDIATION:
<All confirmed issues ordered by severity with remediation steps>

Then: TRIAGE_COMPLETE

HELPFUL COMMANDS
----------------
  show_findings
  cat /task/codebase/app/auth/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/orders/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/payments/app.py
  cat /task/codebase/app/admin/app.py
  tree /task/codebase

==============================================================
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== FINDINGS (Semgrep + CodeQL, 18 total) ===" && cat /task/sarif/findings.sarif | jq -r ".runs[0].results[] | \"Finding \" + (.properties.finding_index | tostring) + \" [\" + (.properties.tool) + \" \" + (.level | ascii_upcase) + \"]: \" + .ruleId + \"
  \" + .locations[0].physicalLocation.region.snippet.text + \"
\""
echo "=============================================="
#!/bin/bash
cat /task/instructions/task.txt

Tier: Ceiling  |  Expected human completion time: ~3 hours

CONTEXT
-------
You are a senior AppSec engineer conducting a full security review of
FinVault, a payment processing platform. TWO SAST tools have been run:
Semgrep and CodeQL. Their output contains 18 findings across 4 services.

Several findings appear twice — once per tool with different severity
ratings. You must resolve every conflict.

  - PCI DSS Level 1 scope (handles real cardholder data)
  - SOX compliance scope (admin and audit service)
  - Financial services regulated environment
